This family consists of flowering plants that are primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions. intro: Fagara shaferi, also known as Mexican prickly ash, is a plant species native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the Rutaceae family. Fagara shaferi has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic effects, making it useful in treating various conditions such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Fagara shaferi include furanocoumarins, alkaloids, and terpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Fagara shaferi is commonly prepared as a tincture, tea, or topical cream, and is often used in traditional remedies for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects.
